svg.title: Gray-Scott grid-size comparison
svg.desc: Same feed-vs-kill scan after 1400 steps on 40x40 and 72x72 lattices. A finite-size check for which cells stay robust in a bigger arena.
svg.text: Same feed-vs-kill scan after 1400 steps on 40x40 and 72x72 lattices. A finite-size check for which cells stay robust in a bigger arena.

text.tspan: The smaller lattice with patch radius 5.
Positive = busier on the larger lattice. Negative = the small box overread occupancy.
The larger lattice with scaled patch radius 9.
Positive = sharper fronts on the larger lattice. Negative = smoothing or box bias.
Biggest larger-lattice growth: F=0.030, k=0.054, Δactive=+1.000.
Biggest larger-lattice fade: F=0.018, k=0.054, Δactive=-0.134.
Sharpest larger-lattice front growth: F=0.018, k=0.051, Δedge=+0.0134.
Strongest larger-lattice smoothing: F=0.022, k=0.060, Δedge=-0.0128.
This is the practical read: some cells are genuinely robust, but others only look settled because the box is small.
The larger lattice mostly confirms the coarse story, then points to the cells where finite-size drift still matters before the repo starts talking like it owns a phase diagram.
